paper_title: Variability of Food Stores of Tetragonisca fiebrigi (Schwarz) (Hymenoptera: Apidae: Meliponini) from the Argentine Chaco Based on Pollen Analysis

methods.description: This method involves the analysis of honey and pollen mass samples from Tetragonisca fiebrigi nests to identify pollen types and their variability across different seasons and forest types.

steps.description: Honeys and pollen masses from the same nest were analyzed separately.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate and prepare honey and pollen mass samples for analysis

steps.description: Honey was sampled from different pots of a nest using a disposable plastic syringe and then homogenized.
steps.searchable_summary: Sample and homogenize honey from nest pots

steps.description: The pollen masses of each nest were mixed and analyzed as an only sample.
Honey and pollen mass samples were dissolved with a glass rod in 200 ml of distilled water at 80-90 ºC and then with a magnetic stirrer for 10-15 minutes.
steps.searchable_summary: Dissolve samples in distilled water and stir

steps.description: Five milliliters of this mixture was centrifuged at 472 g and the sediment was dehydrated using acetic acid and acetolyzed.
Pollen identification was carried out by comparing pollen provision slides with the pollen reference of plants grown in the sites sampled.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify pollen types using reference slides

steps.description: The counting of 500 pollen grains per slide was made for honey samples, while a total of 300-500 grains for pollen masses.

methods.description: This method applies multivariate statistical techniques, including Cluster Analysis and Principal Component Analysis, to analyze the variability and grouping of pollen samples.

steps.description: The Cluster Analysis and Principal Component Analysis were the two multivariate techniques applied.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply Cluster Analysis and PCA to data

steps.description: For Cluster Analysis, the algorithm UPGMA and Bray-Curtis distance were applied to the percentage values of the data matrix in Q-mode.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform UPGMA Cluster Analysis using Bray-Curtis distance

steps.description: For Principal Component Analysis (PCA), a correlation matrix was applied to the data matrix in Q-mode and R-mode.
The Scree plot was used to cut-off the number of significant principal components.
steps.searchable_summary: Use Scree plot to determine significant components
